title: Cycle closure classification: partial vs fully-fed leg vs fully closed
description:
Distinguish discovered cycles by degree of internal closure:

Per-step states:
- Externally fed step — at least one input doesn't appear as output of any earlier step in the cycle. Needs fuel addition.
- Fully fed step — every input is produced by some earlier step. No external addition required for this step.
- Catalytically closed step — every input is both consumed at this step and regenerated by a later step.

Per-cycle classifications:
- Partial closure — catalysts exist (current emit floor) but every step is externally fed. Default.
- Has a fully-fed leg — at least one step is fully fed. Stronger candidate.
- Fully closed — every step is fully fed. True autonomous cycle.

Real example: the 6-step Boron cycle (10B fuel, 20Ne catalyst) is partial closure with one fully-fed leg (step 6: 3He+17O→20Ne, both inputs produced by step 5).

Computation: extend analyzeFlow in CycleVisualization.tsx (already builds producedAt) to emit per-step closure state. Cycle classification falls out as max(step_state).

UI placement options:
- Badge on each cycle row in the results table (Partial / Has fully-fed leg / Fully closed)
- Per-node border in the loop diagram (green for fully-fed step nodes vs current amber-for-feedback)
- Optional sort/filter the results table by closure type
notes:
Related observation 2026-05-11: cycles displayed are often longer than the catalytic closure requires. Algorithm emits cycles at each regeneration but continues DFS deeper, producing longer cycles that include downstream chain steps not contributing to closure. Sort by totalEnergy descending bubbles long cycles to top.

Example: 6-step Boron cycle. Catalytic closure of 20Ne happens at steps {1,2,5,6} (4 reactions). Steps 3 and 4 chain 30Si→40Ar→50Ti (byproduct sink) and don't loop back.

Expand scope to include "cycle minimality":
- Detect non-essential steps (products don't contribute to any catalyst regeneration)
- Options:
  - Truncate emitted cycles at the first regeneration
  - Rank shorter cycles higher (penalize length or use MeV/step)
  - Sort/filter UX for cycle length
  - Lower default maxCycleDepth from 6 to 4 as short-term mitigation
